name: Antalya: Secrets of Kaleiçi Old Town

stops.name: Hadrian's Gate
stops.shortDescription: A monumental triumphal arch built in 130 AD to commemorate the visit of the Roman Emperor Hadrian. It features three beautifully decorated arched gates and serves as the majestic entrance to the old town.

stops.name: Broken Minaret (Kesik Minare)
stops.shortDescription: Originally a Roman temple, converted into a Byzantine church, and later a mosque. The building's iconic minaret was struck by lightning in the 19th century, leaving a jagged 'broken' top that survived for over a century until recent restorations.

stops.name: Hıdırlık Tower
stops.shortDescription: A landmark Roman tower built from yellow stone in the 2nd century AD where the city walls meet the sea. Historians debate whether it was originally built as a lighthouse or a grand mausoleum.

stops.name: Karaalioğlu Park
stops.shortDescription: A lush, expansive park perched directly on the high cliffs over the Mediterranean. The viewing terraces offer breathtaking vistas of the sea and the rugged Taurus Mountains in the distance.

stops.name: Antalya Marina
stops.shortDescription: Nestled in a natural cove, this historic Roman harbor brought great wealth to the ancient city. Today it is filled with traditional wooden gulet boats and surrounded by cafes built into the old city walls.

stops.name: Yivli Minaret Mosque
stops.shortDescription: Constructed by the Seljuk Sultan Alaeddin Keykubat in the 13th century. The unique, elegant fluted minaret, decorated with dark blue tiles, has become the defining symbol of Antalya.

stops.name: Antalya Clock Tower
stops.shortDescription: An impressive stone tower that originally formed part of the ancient city's fortifications. Today, it acts as the primary visual gateway between ancient Kaleiçi and the modern city.

stops.name: Tekeli Mehmet Paşa Mosque
stops.shortDescription: An elegant 18th-century Ottoman mosque situated near the Clock Tower. It features beautiful Arabic calligraphy and colorful, intricate Iznik tilework over its windows and arches.

stops.name: Cumhuriyet Square
stops.shortDescription: The expansive main public square of Antalya, dominated by a massive equestrian monument of Atatürk. It features a dancing fountain and sweeping views over the Mediterranean.

stops.name: Panoramic Glass Elevator
stops.shortDescription: A modern, free-to-use glass elevator built directly into the sheer cliff face. Riding it provides a stunning, effortless transition between the high city center and the old Marina below.

stops.name: Iskele Mosque
stops.shortDescription: A tiny, charming 19th-century mosque located right on the edge of the marina. It rests gracefully on four stone pillars built directly over a natural, flowing spring.

stops.name: Mermerli Beach
stops.shortDescription: A small, secluded historic beach tucked perfectly into the base of the cliffs right next to the Marina. It offers incredibly clear Mediterranean waters for a refreshing dip.

stops.name: Hesapçı Sokak
stops.shortDescription: One of the most picturesque cobblestone streets in Kaleiçi. It is beautifully lined with impeccably restored Ottoman-era mansions featuring traditional wooden overhanging balconies.

stops.name: Kaleiçi Museum
stops.shortDescription: Housed in two beautifully restored historic buildings. This fascinating ethnographic museum provides an immersive look into the traditional daily life and vibrant culture of 19th-century Antalya.
